"**Gator**" **Jackson** (April 25, 1932 – October 25, 1987)[[1]](#cite_note-Dead-1) was an American [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") tenor saxophonist.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American jazz musician (1928–1987)

**Willis** "**Gator**" **Jackson** (April 25, 1932 – October 25, 1987)[[1]](#cite_note-Dead-1) was an American [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") tenor saxophonist.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Willis_Jackson_(saxophonist)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Born in [Miami, Florida](/wiki/Miami,_Florida "Miami, Florida"),[[1]](#cite_note-Dead-1) Jackson joined [Cootie Williams](/wiki/Cootie_Williams "Cootie Williams")'s band in 1948 as a teenager, and was part of it on and off until 1955.[[2]](#cite_note-AM-2)

Under his own name (Willis Jackson and His Orchestra) he recorded various rhythm-and-blues instrumentals for [Atlantic Records](/wiki/Atlantic_Records "Atlantic Records"). His most famous record for Atlantic is "Gator's Groove" (1952), with "Estrellita" as the B-side.

Jackson toured as leader of the backing band for singer [Ruth Brown](/wiki/Ruth_Brown "Ruth Brown"). Publicly they were married, but privately they never married but lived together from 1950 to 1955.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Jackson joined [Prestige Records](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records") in 1959, making a string of albums.[[2]](#cite_note-AM-2)

Jackson died in New York City one week after heart surgery, in October 1987, at the age of 55.[[1]](#cite_note-Dead-1)

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Willis_Jackson_(saxophonist)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Discography")]

### As leader

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Willis_Jackson_(saxophonist)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: As leader")]

* *[Please Mr. Jackson](/wiki/Please_Mr._Jackson "Please Mr. Jackson")* ([Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records"), 1959)
* *[Cool "Gator"](/wiki/Cool_%22Gator%22 "Cool \"Gator\"")* (Prestige, 1959)
* *[Blue Gator](/wiki/Blue_Gator "Blue Gator")* (Prestige, 1960)
* *[Really Groovin'](/wiki/Really_Groovin%27 "Really Groovin'")* (Prestige, 1961)
* *[In My Solitude](/wiki/In_My_Solitude_(Willis_Jackson_album) "In My Solitude (Willis Jackson album)")* (Moodsville, 1961)
* *[Cookin' Sherry](/wiki/Cookin%27_Sherry "Cookin' Sherry")* (Prestige, 1961)
* *[Thunderbird](/wiki/Thunderbird_(Willis_Jackson_album) "Thunderbird (Willis Jackson album)")* (Prestige, 1962)
* *[Bossa Nova Plus](/wiki/Bossa_Nova_Plus "Bossa Nova Plus")* (Prestige, 1963)
* *[Neapolitan Nights](/wiki/Neapolitan_Nights "Neapolitan Nights")* (Prestige, 1963)
* *[Grease 'n' Gravy](/wiki/Grease_%27n%27_Gravy "Grease 'n' Gravy")* (Prestige, 1963)
* *[The Good Life](/wiki/The_Good_Life_(Willis_Jackson_album) "The Good Life (Willis Jackson album)")* (Prestige, 1963)
* *[Loose...](/wiki/Loose... "Loose...")* (Prestige, 1963)
* *[Jackson's Action!](/wiki/Jackson%27s_Action! "Jackson's Action!")* (Prestige, 1964)
* *[Boss Shoutin'](/wiki/Boss_Shoutin%27 "Boss Shoutin'")* (Prestige, 1964)
* *['Gator Tails](/wiki/%27Gator_Tails "'Gator Tails")* ([Verve](/wiki/Verve_Records "Verve Records"), 1964)
* *[More Gravy](/wiki/More_Gravy "More Gravy")* (Prestige, 1964)
* *[Together Again!](/wiki/Together_Again! "Together Again!")* with [Jack McDuff](/wiki/Jack_McDuff "Jack McDuff") (Prestige, 1965)
* *[Smoking with Willis](/wiki/Smoking_with_Willis "Smoking with Willis")* (Cadet, 1965)
* *[Live! Action](/wiki/Live!_Action "Live! Action")* (Prestige, 1966)
* *[Soul Night/Live!](/wiki/Soul_Night/Live! "Soul Night/Live!")* (Prestige, 1966)
* *[Together Again, Again](/wiki/Together_Again,_Again "Together Again, Again")* with Jack McDuff (Prestige, 1967)
* *[Tell It...](/wiki/Tell_It... "Tell It...")* (Prestige, 1967)
* *[Soul Grabber](/wiki/Soul_Grabber "Soul Grabber")* (Prestige, 1968)
* *[Star Bag](/wiki/Star_Bag "Star Bag")* with [Trudy Pitts](/wiki/Trudy_Pitts "Trudy Pitts"), [Bill Jennings](/wiki/Bill_Jennings_(guitarist) "Bill Jennings (guitarist)") (Prestige, 1968)
* *[Swivelhips](/wiki/Swivelhips "Swivelhips")* (Prestige, 1969)
* *[Gator's Groove](/wiki/Gator%27s_Groove "Gator's Groove")* (Prestige, 1969)
* *Mellow Blues* (UpFront, 1970)
* *Recording Session* (Big Chance, 1972)
* *[West Africa](/wiki/West_Africa_(album) "West Africa (album)")* (Muse, 1974)
* *[Headed and Gutted](/wiki/Headed_and_Gutted "Headed and Gutted")* (Muse, 1974)
* *Funky Reggae* (Trip, 1974)
* *The Way We Were* (Atlantic, 1975)
* *Plays with Feeling* (Cotillion, 1976)
* *[In the Alley](/wiki/In_the_Alley "In the Alley")* (Muse, 1977)
* *[Bar Wars](/wiki/Bar_Wars_(album) "Bar Wars (album)")* (Muse, 1978)
* *[The Gator Horn](/wiki/The_Gator_Horn "The Gator Horn")* (Muse, 1979)
* *[Single Action](/wiki/Single_Action_(album) "Single Action (album)")* with [Pat Martino](/wiki/Pat_Martino "Pat Martino") (Muse, 1980)
* *Lockin' Horns* with [Von Freeman](/wiki/Von_Freeman "Von Freeman") (Muse, 1981)
* *[Gatorade](/wiki/Gatorade_(Willis_Jackson_album) "Gatorade (Willis Jackson album)")* (Prestige, 1982)
* *[Nothing Butt...](/wiki/Nothing_Butt... "Nothing Butt...")* (Muse, 1983)
* *Ya Understand Me?* (Muse, 1984)

### As sideman

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Willis_Jackson_(saxophonist)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: As sideman")]

* [George Benson](/wiki/George_Benson "George Benson"), *Erotic Moods* (Paul Winley, 1978)
* [Bo Diddley](/wiki/Bo_Diddley "Bo Diddley"), *[Big Bad Bo](/wiki/Big_Bad_Bo "Big Bad Bo")* (Chess, 1974)
* [Don McLean](/wiki/Don_McLean "Don McLean"), *Homeless Brother* (United Artists, 1974)
* [Johnny "Hammond" Smith](/wiki/Johnny_%22Hammond%22_Smith "Johnny \"Hammond\" Smith"), *[Johnny "Hammond" Cooks with Gator Tail](/wiki/Johnny_%22Hammond%22_Cooks_with_Gator_Tail "Johnny \"Hammond\" Cooks with Gator Tail")* (Prestige, 1962)
